concepts.title: Session Border Controller (SBC) Integration with ZPLS
concepts.summary: An SBC is a customer-provided network device that connects ZPLS to PSTN and third-party PBX systems during outages. Designers should understand this is a distinct setup path from the standard ZPLS configuration — it requires BYOC-P trunk configuration and involves multiple admin portal sections (BYOC Configuration, Route Groups, Routing Rules).
key_points: Maintains PSTN and third-party PBX connectivity during outages
Requires BYOC-P trunk configuration alongside ZPLS
Tested with Audiocodes, Oracle, and Ribbon SBCs

concepts.title: ZPLS Survivability Route Group
concepts.summary: A route group of type 'Survivability' links SBCs to ZPLS modules. It defines how calls are routed during failover. This is a separate concept from standard Zoom Phone route groups — the type field ('Survivability') is the key discriminator.
key_points: Type must be set to 'Survivability'
Associates SBCs with ZPLS modules
Found under BYOC Configuration > Route Groups

concepts.title: ZPLS Emergency Number Pool (ELIN)
concepts.summary: When SBC is configured with ZPLS, an emergency number pool must be set up for survivability mode. A dedicated BYOC number serves as the Emergency Location Identification Number (ELIN) for the site. Designers should note that emergency services configuration is mandatory, not optional, for SBC + ZPLS setups.
key_points: Dedicated BYOC number required as ELIN
ELIN must not be assigned to any user or Zoom Phone entity
Emergency services routing differs in survivability mode

user_roles.title: Account Owner or Admin with Edit Privileges
user_roles.summary: SBC integration requires admin access across multiple admin portal sections: BYOC Configuration (Number Management), Route Groups, and Routing Rules. This is a cross-functional setup that spans telephony and network administration domains.
key_points: Requires access to both Number Management and account settings
Must configure SBC, route groups, routing rules, and emergency numbers
Multiple Sites must be enabled for Zoom Phone

task_flows.title: Integrate SBC with ZPLS
task_flows.summary: Step 1: Navigate Number Management > BYOC Configuration > SBCs > Manage > select SBC > enter Survivability IP Address > Save. The IP address field is critical — for most deployments, this is the SBC's internal (RFC1918) IP.
key_points: Path: Number Management > BYOC Configuration > SBCs > Manage
Enter SBC's internal IP address (not public IP)
Ports and protocols must be allowed per firewall requirements

task_flows.title: Create Survivability Route Group
task_flows.summary: Navigate Number Management > BYOC Configuration > Route Groups > Manage > Add. Set Display Name, Type = 'Survivability', select SBCs. Key UX note: the type dropdown must include 'Survivability' as a distinct option from normal route group types.
key_points: Path: Number Management > BYOC Configuration > Route Groups > Manage > Add
Type must be 'Survivability' (not standard route group type)
Options Ping should be enabled on SBC for connectivity monitoring

constraints.title: SBC Auto-Failover Triggers
constraints.summary: SBC must be configured to redirect to ZPLS when: (1) OPTIONS ping to both Zoom primary and secondary destinations fail, or (2) Zoom Cloud returns SIP 503. These are network-level conditions invisible to admin UI, but the failover status should be surfaced in the admin portal.
key_points: Two failover triggers: OPTIONS ping failure OR SIP 503
SBC configuration is external to Zoom admin portal
Failover status should be visible in admin UI

constraints.title: SBC Connectivity Requirements
constraints.summary: Single node: SBC configured with ZPLS node IP. Node group: SBC must route to at least 2 ZPLS node IPs to avoid single point of failure. This constraint varies based on whether a node group is used.
key_points: Single node: 1 IP required
Node group: minimum 2 IPs required for redundancy
Uses internal (RFC1918) IP addresses

constraints.title: ZPLS Routing Rules Limitation
constraints.summary: During survivability mode, routing rules' Routing Path (SIP Group or PSTN) is ignored — only number translations are preserved. 'Other sites' routing path is limited to same-site extension-to-extension calls only. Designers need to communicate these degraded-mode behaviors.
key_points: Routing Path ignored in survivability mode
Only number translations preserved
Cross-site calls via 'Other sites' path not supported in survivability

ux_patterns.title: Multi-Section Admin Configuration Flow
ux_patterns.summary: SBC + ZPLS setup spans 5+ admin portal sections (SBCs, Route Groups, Routing Rules, Emergency Numbers, Outbound Caller ID). This is a complex cross-cutting configuration that would benefit from a unified setup wizard or status dashboard showing completion state across sections.
key_points: Spans Number Management, Phone System Management, and account settings
No single dashboard for ZPLS+SBC setup status
Setup order matters: SBC → Route Group → Routing Rules → Emergency Pool
